title: BitWriter.to_bytes fails silently on extremely large bit counts
description:
File: python/src/lichen/schc/codec.py:75-81

BitWriter accumulates bits in self._acc (a Python int) and self._nbits (count). The to_bytes() method converts to bytes:
  def to_bytes(self) -> bytes:
      if self._nbits == 0:
          return b''
      pad = (-self._nbits) % 8
      total = self._nbits + pad
      return (self._acc << pad).to_bytes(total // 8, 'big')

Python ints have arbitrary precision, so _acc can grow unbounded. However, if total // 8 exceeds memory, this would raise OverflowError or MemoryError.

In practice, SCHC residues are small (tens of bytes), but there's no explicit limit preventing misuse. A malicious or buggy caller could write() billions of bits before calling to_bytes().

Fix: Consider adding a maximum accumulated bits check, or document the expected usage bounds.

Category: CORRECTNESS
close_reason: Added explicit bounds check and test verifying OverflowError for excessive bit counts. Matches bead description.
